seoTitle: Fargunyah vs Farmborough Heights — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Fargunyah and Farmborough Heights. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Fargunyah vs Farmborough Heights: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fargunyah and Farmborough Heights.
heading: Fargunyah vs Farmborough Heights — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fargunyah, New South Wales and Farmborough Heights, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Farmborough Heights has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Fargunyah (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fargunyah or Farmborough Heights safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fargunyah has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Farmborough Heights scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fargunyah scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Farmborough Heights scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fargunyah or Farmborough Heights?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Farmborough Heights has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
